summary: Phase 2 runs two research subtasks in parallel with a shared 2-week time budget (LLM-leveraged, not sequential), each producing a committed deliverable that is itself part of the research contribution. (1) Scorer design document defining measurement dimensions for sustained-codebase work: regression rate, rework ratio, decision compliance, context reconstitution cost, architectural coherence across sprints, plus candidate new dimensions surfaced by research. New scorers are built rather than reusing existing ones
context: Committed Phase 2 gates: scorer design doc + methodology doc, parallel, 2-week time-budget. Named fixture-branching fallback risk explicitly.
rationale: existing scorers are calibrated for 12-session scenarios, not multi-sprint accumulation. (2) Methodology document defining fixture-branching design, statistical treatment of non-independent branched runs, mixed-rigor claim boundaries, and power analysis for the flagship scenario. External review is optional; time-to-commitment is the binding constraint. Known risk: fixture-branching assumes end-of-sprint-1 state differs meaningfully across conditions. If twining-default and shared-markdown produce similar sprint-1 states, single-fixture branching collapses and per-condition fixtures become necessary, roughly halving cost savings. The methodology document must address this and state the per-condition fallback plan before the flagship run starts. Rationale: naming research time
constraints: 2-week total wall-clock time budget for Phase 2
Methodology document must specify a per-condition-fixture fallback plan before flagship run starts
Scorers must be calibrated for multi-sprint accumulation, not 12-session scenario horizons

alternatives.option: sequential), each producing a committed deliverable that is itself part of the research contribution. (1) Scorer design document defining measurement dimensions for sustained-codebase work: regression rate, rework ratio, decision compliance, context reconstitution cost, architectural coherence across sprints, plus candidate new dimensions surfaced by research. New scorers are built rather than reusing existing ones

assumptions: LLM-leveraged parallel work on the two docs fits within 2 weeks wall-clock
End-of-sprint-1 state differs meaningfully across conditions (required for single-fixture branching to be valid)
Deliverable-gated research produces stronger output than open-loop exploration for this kind of methodology work
